data.title: Market Analysis: Gold and silver prices rose after the US-Iran ceasefire, mainly due to improved market sentiment and reduced bets on interest rate hikes.
data.description: A report by the financial website Investinglive states that, typically, easing geopolitical tensions are detrimental to precious metals such as gold and silver. However, this time the opposite has occurred. Before the outbreak of the US-Iran conflict, gold and silver positions relied heavily on leveraged trading. The negative impact of the conflict led to leveraged selling, causing precious metals like gold and silver to fall. After the US-Iran ceasefire, improved market sentiment drove a rebound in gold and silver prices. Furthermore, the US-Iran ceasefire also means that major central banks may not need to raise interest rates too aggressively. This is also one of the reasons for the rebound in precious metals. (Jinshi)

data.title: CryptoQuant: Bitcoin active addresses fall to an 8-year low, potentially signaling a long-term accumulation phase.
data.description: According to CryptoQuant data, active Bitcoin addresses have fallen to an 8-year low, indicating reduced speculative activity and a potential long-term accumulation phase.

data.title: Pansen Macro: Despite the decline in oil prices, the market may still face significant inflationary shocks in the future.
data.description: Pansen Macroeconomist stated that although oil prices fell after the two-week ceasefire agreement, the impending inflationary shock will still be significant. He said, "The ceasefire agreement eliminates the prospect of an April rate hike, which we have always considered an unlikely scenario, but we maintain our view that the ECB will tighten policy slightly this summer." Despite market welcome of the ceasefire, Iran may still restrict traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. He added, "Tehran has learned from this crisis that it can exert significant pressure on the United States by controlling the strait, and we see no reason why Iran wouldn't take advantage of this now." (Jinshi)

data.title: Economist Warns of Upcoming Inflation Shock Despite Oil Price Drop
data.description: Pantheon Macroeconomics economist has indicated that despite the recent decline in oil prices following a two-week ceasefire agreement, a significant inflation shock is anticipated. According to Jin10, the economist stated that the ceasefire agreement has eliminated the prospect of an interest rate hike in April, which was always considered unlikely. However, the view remains that the European Central Bank will slightly tighten its policy during the summer. While the market has welcomed the ceasefire, Iran may still restrict traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. The economist added that Tehran has learned from this crisis that it can exert substantial pressure on the United States by controlling the strait, and there is no reason for Iran not to leverage this advantage now.

data.title: Pundi X Collaborates with Fromm.io to Develop AI Financial Infrastructure
data.description: Pundi X (PUNDIX) has announced a collaboration with Fromm.io to establish financial infrastructure tailored for AI agents. According to NS3.AI, Pundi AI will focus on offering data and model access, while Fromm.io will facilitate micro-payments, escrow services, and automated settlements. This partnership aims to enhance the capabilities of AI agents in managing financial transactions efficiently.

data.title: MetaPlanet CEO: The company has over 250,000 shareholders in Japan.
data.description: Simon Gerovich, CEO of Bitcoin treasury company MetaPlanet, announced that the company now has over 250,000 shareholders in Japan, making it one of the most held stocks in the country. (BitcoinTreasuries.NET)

data.title: Iran Considers Toll for Hormuz Strait Passage
data.description: On April 8, Iran's Ambassador to China, Fazli, addressed concerns regarding potential tolls for passage through the Hormuz Strait. According to BlockBeats, Fazli stated in Beijing that Iran has not yet established an official toll scheme or standards. The decision will be based on current global waterway toll standards and international law.

data.title: MetaPlanet's Japanese Shareholders Surpass 250,000
data.description: MetaPlanet CEO Simon Gerovich announced that the number of Japanese shareholders in the company has exceeded 250,000. According to Odaily, this milestone makes MetaPlanet one of the most widely held stocks in Japan.

data.title: Bitget launches a special return event for existing users, with a total prize pool of 100,000 USDT.
data.description: Bitget is launching a special return promotion for existing users who haven't traded contracts in the past 30 days or earlier. During the promotion period, completing tasks related to contract trading volume and cumulative trading will earn users a limited number of scratch card chances, up to a maximum of 10. The prize pool is worth 100,000 USDT, covering trial vouchers, trial funds, and popular tokens such as XAUT, XRP, and SOL. The maximum prize per scratch is 0.3 XAUT. Detailed rules have been published on the official Bitget platform. Users can register by clicking the "Join Now" button to participate. The promotion ends on April 21, 2026 at 23:59 (UTC+8).

data.title: ECB's Dolenc Warns of Inflation and Growth Risks Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: European Central Bank (ECB) Governing Council member Dolenc has expressed concerns about the potential negative impact of the ongoing conflict in Iran on inflation and economic growth. According to Jin10, Dolenc highlighted that the continuation of the war could pose significant challenges to the economic stability of the region. The conflict's influence on energy prices and supply chains is particularly worrisome, as it may lead to increased inflationary pressures and hinder economic recovery efforts. Dolenc's remarks underscore the importance of monitoring geopolitical developments and their implications for the European economy.

data.title: South Korea Implements Enhanced Withdrawal Delay System for Virtual Assets
data.description:
South Korea's Financial Services Commission (FSC) and Financial Supervisory Service (FSS) have announced the implementation of an enhanced withdrawal delay system in collaboration with the Digital Asset Exchange Association (DAXA) and various virtual asset exchanges. According to Foresight News, the authorities have strengthened the criteria for withdrawal delay exceptions and established unified internal standards.

The new system aims to prevent telecommunication fraud by restricting certain groups, such as new users, from withdrawing virtual assets within a specified period. Previously, the lack of clear benchmarks and inconsistent standards across exchanges allowed criminals to exploit these loopholes. Statistics indicate that from June to September last year, 59% of fraud accounts at virtual asset exchanges were classified as 'exception accounts' under the withdrawal delay system.

data.title: Iranian Parliament Speaker to Lead Delegation for U.S.-Iran Talks in Islamabad
data.description: Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf is set to lead a delegation to participate in U.S.-Iran negotiations in Islamabad, Pakistan, on April 10. According to Odaily, this development follows confirmation from Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif that Iran, the United States, and their respective allies have agreed to an immediate ceasefire in all locations, including Lebanon and other regions, effective immediately. The talks aim to reach a final agreement to resolve all disputes.

data.title: Swiss Banks to Test Digital Franc by 2026
data.description: Six Swiss banks, including UBS, the largest bank in Switzerland, are planning to test a digital version of the Swiss franc by 2026. According to ChainCatcher, this initiative aims to explore the practical applications of a digital currency in real banking and financial scenarios.

data.title: Swiss Banks Collaborate on Testing Swiss Franc Stablecoin Use Cases
data.description: UBS and five other Swiss banks are collaborating to test use cases for a Swiss franc stablecoin. According to Odaily, this initiative aims to explore the potential applications and benefits of stablecoins within the Swiss financial system. The collaboration reflects a growing interest in digital currencies and their integration into traditional banking operations.

data.title: CertiK's AI Auditor Identifies 86.6% of Vulnerabilities in Web3 Security Test
data.description: CertiK has introduced AI Auditor, an AI-driven audit tool that demonstrated its capability by identifying 86.6% of vulnerabilities in a test involving 35 Web3 security incidents from this year. According to NS3.AI, the tool is designed to integrate security analysis directly into development workflows. CertiK aims to further expand the AI Auditor's application into developer tools, compliance systems, and institutional monitoring frameworks.

data.title: Bitcoin Exchange Balances Decline as Holders Withdraw
data.description:
Bitcoin balances on exchanges have been steadily decreasing, according to recent data from CryptoQuant. According to BlockBeats, over the past year, the amount of Bitcoin held on trading platforms has dropped by approximately 100,000 BTC, leaving around 867,000 BTC still stored on exchanges.

Analysts suggest that Bitcoin continues to benefit from holders who prefer to withdraw their assets from exchanges rather than repeatedly reintroducing them to the market.

data.title: UBS partnered with five other Swiss banks to test use cases for the Swiss franc stablecoin.
data.description: According to Reuters, UBS is collaborating with five other Swiss banks to test use cases for the Swiss franc stablecoin.

data.title: North Korean Hackers: A Growing Concern in Cybersecurity
data.description: Foresight News posted on X (formerly Twitter) highlighting the increasing threat posed by North Korean hackers in the cybersecurity landscape. The post underscores the importance of vigilance against cyber threats originating from North Korea, which have been linked to various high-profile cyber attacks globally. As cybersecurity remains a critical issue, experts emphasize the need for enhanced security measures to protect sensitive information from such malicious actors.

data.title: Switzerland's March Unadjusted Unemployment Rate at 3.1%
data.description: Switzerland's unadjusted unemployment rate for March was reported at 3.1%, according to Jin10. This figure aligns with expectations and shows a slight decrease from the previous rate of 3.2%.

data.title: STOCKS | Global Markets Surge Following U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Agreement
data.description:
Global stock markets experienced significant gains following the announcement of a two-week ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran. According to Odaily, the Taiwan Weighted Index rose by 4.61% to close at 34,761.38 points. Japan's Nikkei 225 Index saw an increase of 5.39%, closing at 56,308.42 points, while South Korea's KOSPI Index climbed 6.87% to 5,872.34 points.

The MSCI Asia Pacific Index expanded its gains to 5%, with Sri Lanka's All Share Price Index rising by 4.2%. In India, both the SENSEX and NIFTY indices increased by 3.7%. European markets also showed positive movement, with the Euro Stoxx 50 futures up 5.3%, Germany's DAX futures rising 5.2%, and the FTSE 100 futures gaining 2.9%.
